titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sir John Thomas Duckworth Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid is written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 28, 2024 at 00:06

did.unittitle: Sir John Thomas Duckworth papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, letter books, printed material, artwork, commemorative medals, and other papers by or relating to British Admiral Sir John Thomas Duckworth.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of James M. Osborn, 1972. Purchased from Sotheby's via Winifred Meyers, 1972. Purchased from William P. Wreden, 1974. Purchased from Marine & Cannon Books on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. March 1972 Acquisition [1 of 2], 1739-1908. II. March 1972 Acquisition [2 of 2], 1807-1812. III. March 1974 Acquisition, 1777-1908. IV. October 2008 Acquisition, 1790-1817.

bioghist.head: Sir John Thomas Duckworth (1748-1817)
bioghist.p: Sir John Thomas Duckworth, 1st Baronet, was a British officer in the Royal Navy, serving during the Seven Years' War, the American Revolutionary War, and the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic wars. He was appointed commander-in-chief of Barbados and the Leeward Islands Station in 1800, commander-in-chief of the Jamaica Station in 1803, second in command of the Mediterranean Fleet in 1805, and commander of the Channel Station in 1808. In 1810, he achieved the rank of Admiral of the Blue and served as governor and commander-in-chief of Newfoundland from 1810 to 1812.

Duckworth was created a baronet in November 1813. He married Ann (or Anne) Wallis in 1776, and they had a son and daughter. In 1808, he married Susannah Catherine Buller. They had two sons, including John Thomas Buller Duckworth.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sir John Thomas Duckworth Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, letter books, printed material, artwork, commemorative medals, and other papers by or relating to British Admiral Sir John Thomas Duckworth. Materials document British naval strategy and operations during the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic wars, including the Dardanelles operation and Alexandria expedition in 1807. The collection contains orders and instructions to naval commanders from the British Admiralty and papers concerning privateering, impressment of sailors, courts-martial, dissemination of signal books, and other military matters. Among the correspondents are British Secretary at War Henry Dundas, Royal Navy officers John Gambier and John Jervis, British officer of arms Isaac Heard, and Duckworth's wife Susannah. Artwork includes an engraving of Duckworth.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sir John Thomas Duckworth Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: March 1972 Acquisition [1 of 2]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, orders, commissions, and other papers relating to Sir John Thomas Duckworth's service during the French Revolutionary Wars and Napoleonic Wars, as well as his governorship of Newfoundland in the early part of the War of 1812. Materials primarily concern military and naval engagements, privateering, courts-martial, and supplies for ships. Included are references to Duckworth's involvement in operations in the West Indies and the British assault on Constantinople in 1807.

did.unittitle: Account book
Account of British naval operations near Constantinople [incomplete]
Account of convalescents supplied with refreshments on the ship Centaur
Account of Mediterranean passes issued in 1812
Account of passes issued in 1810
Account of ships of war and merchantmen captured or detained by HMS Glatton
Additional Article to the Treaty concluded at Chaumont [printed circular]
Answer to the articles for new naval system
Articles of capitulation related to British expedition to Alexandria during Anglo-Turkish War [in Italian]
Autograph manuscript copy of Parliamentary laws
Bonaparte and the Queen of Prussia [account of their meeting in unknown hand]
Captains &c. Letter Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters reference seizure of vessels during the Anglo-Turkish war, returns of wounded and killed sailors, courts-martial, military actions during the Napoleonic Wars and War of 1812, and Duckworth's administration of affairs in Newfoundland.

Financial records [primarily related to Royal Navy]

Letter book
Letter book - Newfoundland
Letter book/Order book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of letters, memoranda, and orders relating to the war with France while Duckworth was a naval commander. A receipt and letter from 1806 and 1809 are laid in to the volume.

did.unittitle: Letter book of private letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters relating to the HMS Leviathan and French Revolutionary Wars.

did.unittitle: Letter book with blank printed orders laid in
Letter book with unidentified account and copies of Admiralty letters laid in
Memoranda and orders
Minutes of facts and opinions … [concerning] controversy between Sir John Thomas Duckworth and Sir Robert Calder
Minutes of proceedings of a court-martial
Mongkut, King of Siam. Circulars regarding son's illness and death
Newfoundland - orders, directions, correspondence, and other documents by Sir John Thomas Duckworth
Order book/Letter book, HMS Leviathan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of letters from the commander in chief of the French navy to the English commander in chief at Môle-Saint-Nicolas in Haiti, in which he requests that French prisoners of war be treated fairly and that British forces permit two Black women to travel to Cape François with the cartel.

did.unittitle: Parliamentary resolutions
Printed orders and other forms, blank
Printed orders and other forms, blank
Records related to Royal Navy ships and sailors
Request for transfer of Dudley Gordon King
Survey of carpenter's stores
Unidentified table of contents
Verses for George III
Waterhouse, Benjamin. Instructions to Mr. Peirce as to granting parole
Weekly returns of provisions on Royal Navy ships, blank manuscript forms
March 1972 Acquisition [2 of 2]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of one letter book documenting Sir John Thomas Duckworth's position as second in command of the Mediterranean Station, commander of the Channel Station, and governor and commander-in-chief of Newfoundland. Letters frequently reference supplying ships, courts-martial and punishment of sailors, privateering, and security of British fisheries during the War of 1812. Included are copies of orders concerning the Anglo-Turkish War and rules relating to the annual British fishing convoy, as well as instructions to the crews of fishing vessels respecting interactions with Indigenous people in Newfoundland.

did.unittitle: Letter book
March 1974 Acquisition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This bulk of this series consists of autograph letters to Sir John Thomas Duckworth from other British naval commanders and superior officers, business and legal associates, and friends. Also included are orders, commissions, warrants, financial accounts, and printed material. Letters and documents primarily concern capture of enemy vessels and cargo by British ships and disposition of prize money. Additional topics are military engagements during the French Revolutionary Wars and Napoleonic Wars, courts-martial of sailors, and surveying and supplying ships.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Athill, Richard O., with statement of crew on captured Danish ship Viena
Baker, Henry
Baker, Sophia
Baker, William

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters concern military, political, diplomatic, and personal matters. Included are references to privateering, British occupation of Surinam (Dutch Guiana) and Indigenous people in the territory, the Irish rebellion of 1803, the Haitian Revolution, the sale of Louisiana to the United States, and Napoleon Bonaparte's coronation. Among the correspondents are Duckworth, William Henry Cavendish-Bentinck, William Windham, Cyril Jackson, and John Jervis.

October 2008 Acquisition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, printed material, artwork, and other papers by or relating to Sir John Thomas Duckworth. Materials document Duckworth's time serving in the West Indies and as Governor of Newfoundland and his personal life. Included are various military orders, cargo lists, acts regarding military discipline, and documents detailing intelligence matters. Also included are British newspapers; an engraved portrait of Duckworth; an engraving depicting a ship firing a salute in St. John's, Newfoundland; and two commemorative medals, one silver and one copper, depicting a bas-relief portrait of Duckworth on the obverse and an inscription on the reverse that reads: "Dedicated by the followers to the memory of their illustrious commander MDCCCXVII." The series further contains an illuminated manuscript installing Duckworth as a baronet in 1814, signed by Sir Isaac Heard, and letters to Duckworth from his wife, Susannah Catherine Duckworth.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.
